title: 250,000 Indian farmers and students march across Delhi and Mumbai against GMO mandates
summary: An estimated 250,000 protesters marched across Delhi and Mumbai combining farmer opposition to GMO crop mandates with student protests over job quotas. Highway blockades disrupted transportation, prompting government offers of dialogue.
contents:
# India: 250,000 March in Combined Farmer-Student Protest Movement

## Protest Scale
Approximately 250,000 protesters assembled across Delhi, Mumbai, and other major Indian cities on May 1, 2026, combining May Day labor demonstrations with specific policy grievances.

## Key Demands
- Farmers oppose mandatory GMO crop cultivation policies
- Students protest job quota system reforms
- Labor unions demand improved working conditions and wages
- Climate activists joined demanding environmental protections

## Disruption
Protesters established highway blockades across multiple states, disrupting transportation and commerce. The blockades affected major trade corridors connecting northern and western India.

## Government Response
The Indian government offered talks with protest leaders, seeking to defuse tensions before further escalation. Previous farmer protest movements in India demonstrated the political potency of agricultural grievances.

## Tags
india, protests, may-day, farmers, students, delhi, mumbai, gmo, labor
